 {"id":"bd-2auv","title":"Feature: Pro Cost Estimator + Budget Alerts (Web + CLI)","description":"## Background
Pro users need predictable costs and time budgeting. They want to see estimated token usage and set monthly caps before running prompts.

## Scope
- Deterministic token + cost estimator per prompt + model (web + CLI).
- UI badges showing estimated tokens and cost; compare estimate vs actual after runs.
- Budget configuration (monthly cap + per-run threshold) with alerts.
- CLI: `jfp cost <prompt>` and `jfp run --estimate`, plus budget status in `jfp status`.
- Prompts/prompt packs only (no SKILL.md or skills content).

## Deliverables
- Shared pricing table + cost calculation utility.
- UI components + settings page for budgets and alerts.
- CLI commands and Pro-gated upgrade messaging.
- Alert logging/audit trail for over-budget events.

## Test Requirements
### Unit Tests
- Cost calculation across models and price tiers.
- Budget threshold logic and alert formatting.

### Integration Tests
- Estimator uses prompt variables and produces stable output.
- Budget updates persist and are read by CLI.

### E2E Tests
- Set budget -> estimate -> exceed -> alert shown in UI and CLI.

## Success Criteria
- Estimates within acceptable tolerance vs actual token usage.
- Users can see cost before running prompts.
- Budget alerts prevent surprise overages.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:13:36.190844227Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T23:05:13.792240399Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T21:04:28.450652742Z","close_reason":"Implemented in 116012f (budget settings UI + CLI budget alerts); backend persistence/hard-stop tracked separately","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":16,"issue_id":"bd-2auv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Web UI cost badges implemented in commit fc824eb. CostBadge added to PromptCard and PromptDetailModal. Remaining: budget settings page and alert logging.","created_at":"2026-01-31T06:33:35Z"},{"id":17,"issue_id":"bd-2auv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Session 2026-01-31: Added CostBadge tests (commit 3e83b39). Web badges complete. Remaining work: budget settings page requires backend/database infrastructure.","created_at":"2026-01-31T06:46:06Z"},{"id":18,"issue_id":"bd-2auv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added budget alert log summary to jfp status (JSON + human output). Pending tests (requires explicit delete approval).","created_at":"2026-02-01T21:09:50Z"},{"id":21,"issue_id":"bd-2auv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Updated registry status tests to assert budgetAlerts in JSON output (tests not executed due to deletion guard).","created_at":"2026-02-01T21:17:11Z"},{"id":23,"issue_id":"bd-2auv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added CLI budget alert log viewer (jfp cost --alerts/--alerts-limit), web budget page import + alert history + CLI sync commands, and docs updates (README + CLI help + web CLI help). Tests not run due to deletion guard.","created_at":"2026-02-01T23:05:13Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2txr","title":"E2E Tests: History UI Components","description":"## Task
Add UI-level E2E tests for the Recently Viewed History feature.

## Context
The API-level E2E tests exist (e2e/history/recently-viewed.spec.ts) but UI component tests are missing.

## Test Scenarios
1. **RecentlyViewedSidebar Component**
   - Sidebar displays recent items
   - Clear history button works
   - Items link to correct pages
   - Empty state displays correctly
   - Loading state shows correctly

2. **History Page (/history)**
   - Page loads and displays items
   - Search/filter works
   - Clear all history works
   - Timestamps display correctly
   - Links navigate correctly

3. **History Tracking Integration**
   - Viewing a prompt adds to history
   - Search queries are tracked
   - Deduplication prevents rapid duplicates
   - Items appear in sidebar after viewing

## Files to Modify
- e2e/history/recently-viewed.spec.ts (add UI tests)
- e2e/lib/history-helpers.ts (add UI selectors)

## Success Criteria
- All sidebar interactions tested
- History page fully tested
- Integration with tracking verified","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralStar","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:45:40.323914674Z","created_by":"CoralStar","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:54:46.005730906Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:54:46.005649042Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":13,"issue_id":"bd-2txr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation complete:
- Added 16 new UI tests (34 total including API tests)
- History Page UI tests: header, empty state, items display, search filter, clear button, navigation
- RecentlyViewedSidebar tests: title, empty state, history link, items display, 8-item limit
- History Tracking Integration tests: cross-page sync, clear sync, search query display
- Updated history-helpers.ts with better UI selectors for sidebar and history page
- 68 total tests (34 × 2 browser configs: chromium + Mobile Chrome)","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:54:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vsu","title":"Feature: Personalized Recommendations Feed (Pro)","description":"## Background
Pro users want to discover high‑value prompts quickly without browsing the entire catalog. A personalized feed drives engagement and retention.

## Scope
- "For You" feed based on usage signals (views, runs, saves) + explicit preferences.
- Explanations: "Because you used X" or "Trending in your category".
- Weekly digest email (opt‑in) with top recommendations.
- CLI: `jfp recommend` returns a ranked list with rationale.
- Prompts/prompt packs only (no SKILL.md or skills content).

## Deliverables
- Ranking pipeline and data model for signals.
- Feed UI with filters (timeframe, category).
- Digest email template + scheduling job.
- CLI command with JSON output.

## Test Requirements
### Unit Tests
- Ranking weight calculations and deterministic ordering.
- Preference filtering and exclusion rules.

### Integration Tests
- Signal ingestion -> feed updates -> UI reflects changes.
- Digest job generates consistent results.

### E2E Tests
- Toggle preferences -> feed updates -> digest preview.
- CLI recommend -> matches feed top items.

## Success Criteria
- Users find relevant prompts faster.
- Feed is explainable and feels trustworthy.
- Opt‑in privacy settings are respected.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:14:02.330248162Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:30:55.979101810Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
